name: The White House
subject: Giving Our Veterans the Care They Deserve
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cfce642952224b3eb7c6fe07e601bbac885c7cf124b077f81ee3c7897461f1ee4d633389b478b20f1e08adf6ee9bf246817c2fa161cc760c

The President's Weekly Address

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cfce642952224b3ed53217a9f228090f318e4d49665e5a29e0294f20b3889f100a0f2f095b787ae6cafe069b7ae8052bfc7452d14ae99936

-------------

Giving Our Veterans the Care They Deserve

Yesterday, President Trump and Secretary David Shulkin announced three initiatives that will expand access to health care for Veterans across the country. Using telehealth technology and mobile applications, the VA will connect with more Veterans to provide services where they live.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cfce642952224b3ea2e2c25816148664a4d736e87015c29c3b87462feb673d2e19499a8a7b36a09f7f643647e2aee51d2e1b7240611719f8
Watch the announcement

Vice President Pence’s Trip to Europe

Vice President Pence has returned from Eastern Europe after a successful week advancing President Trump's America First agenda and strengthening our partnerships in the region.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cfce642952224b3ececdfa08858263b85cc5505ec3671afc3ab3de4898b4f8992d53fe914c2df03176f14c33f9d41c3e8f3c92f40af141f8
Watch the video

Ivanka Trump and Kellyanne Conway Host Listening Session With Military Spouses

Ivanka Trump and Kellyanne Conway, along with Secretary Acosta and Administrator McMahon, hosted a listening session with military spouses from various branches of the military, discussing the challenges they face with finding and retaining employment

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cfce642952224b3ee68884be819619e9058e6f3080899e620284373d1f2513479d380df8383008ae6cf4530bd0199c17a70dcc756c4cf05a
Read more

Photo of the Day

Marine One | August 3, 2017 (Official White House Shealah Craighead)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump and Vice President Pence will participate in a briefing on Hurricane Season at FEMA Headquarters, followed by lunch together at the White House. Later this evening, the Vice President will deliver remarks to Young America’s Foundation 39th Annual National Conservative Student Conference in D.C.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cfce642952224b3e36755aba51c402211e9c454b8f30d170f0b3c1e62e9f9cf6b54b6897345dfd5eb03bc9ecc0b2eed75e5fece3b757d93c
Privacy Policy |

Contact the White House |

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Proud to support Justin
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

I'm proud to support Justin Fairfax in his campaign to be Virginia's next Lieutenant Governor.

Justin's commitment to economic security for all families is matched only by his work ethic. That work ethic allowed him, the youngest child of a single mom without much money, to graduate from Duke University and Columbia Law School.

After graduating, Justin went on to serve in all three branches of government, including as an Assistant U.S. Attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia and the Deputy Coordinator of the Northern Virginia Human Trafficking Task Force.

**As someone who's done the job, I know Justin's depth and breadth of experience makes him the right choice to serve as Virginia 's next Lieutenant Governor. [Will you chip in to support his campaign, Derek?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=EXyiJMAj6vh5d9nphLpCcJmXE1h%2FJzqA)**

Justin's top priority is bringing high-paying jobs to our Commonwealth and strengthening the middle class. As a small business owner, he's committed to ensuring an environment where businesses can hire and thrive. He's also a fierce advocate for criminal justice reform and college affordability.

In these challenging times, Virginia needs leaders who are committed to ensuring a better quality of life for all of our neighbors. We can count on Justin to be a caring, empathetic Lieutenant Governor, who honors his role as a public servant. **[I hope you'll join me in supporting him by making a donation today.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=BMgdaprYR7%2BoXjqZ%2BEQ17ZmXE1h%2FJzqA)**

Thank you,

Tim

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: When voting rights expanded [video]
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

On August 6, 1965, President Johnson signed the Voting Rights Act into
law, one of the seminal achievements in the history of our democracy.

That anniversary is coming up in just two days. But instead of
celebrating everyone’s equal voice in our democracy this Sunday, we
will face a democracy rapidly losing its legitimacy as it is
undermined by voter ID laws, hyperpartisan gerrymandering, felon
disenfranchisement, and more.

That’s why Russ Feingold and thousands of members of LegitAction have
joined together to support a new 21st Century Voting Rights Act.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1002&akid=189%2E356062%2EhqG3KG ]Watch this short video making the case for the 21st Century Voting
Rights Act today, then add your name to the petition to show your
support for reform.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1003&akid=189%2E356062%2EhqG3KG ]Russ Feingold makes the case for the 21st Century Voting Rights Act

The right to vote is not a luxury or a reward, handed out by the
government as it sees fit. It is the most fundamental right of any
democracy, granting it legitimacy by instilling power in the people
and not in politicians.

Today, that fundamental right -- and that legitimacy -- are under
assault.

Courts this year found North Carolina’s hyperpartisan gerrymandering
targeted African-American voters “with almost surgical precision.”
Acquiring an ID to vote costs more today, even adjusting for
inflation, than poll taxes in the 60’s. And felon disenfranchisement
now bars 1 in 13 Black folks from their right to vote nationwide.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1004&akid=189%2E356062%2EhqG3KG ]We must keep building momentum to fix the voting rights abuses of
2017 with a 21st Century Voting Rights Act. Watch the video today,
then sign the petition.

This is a bold thing to demand at a time when it seems like the deck
is more stacked against us in Washington than ever. But solving big
challenges requires bold solutions, and we can’t let progressives and
Democrats in Congress turn away from that.

Thank you for supporting this important push,

Russ Feingold

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1005&akid=189%2E356062%2EhqG3KG ]TAKE ACTION »

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Do no harm
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|

[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2735/7377f838/375a9ed1/51fbd9e9/3812481161/VEsH/ "http://www.joeforindiana.com/")

| | Before I say anything else, I want to thank you, Peter. I'm proud to say that last week I was part of a bipartisan group of Senators that came together to stop the repeal of the Affordable Care Act. When I was on the Senate floor voting to protect health care for hundreds of thousands of Hoosiers, I knew you had my back -- and that meant the world to me.

But the fight to protect our health care isn't done yet. Out of spite, the Administration is now indicating that it may try to throw health insurance markets into a tailspin by ending crucial payments to the states that lower deductibles and co-pays.

The American people have spoken, and our message should continue to be clear: **Now is not the time to play political games with people's health insurance.[Will you sign my petition to demand that the Trump Administration drop its threat to destabilize our insurance market?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2735/7377f838/375a9ed1/51fbd9e8/3812481161/VEsE/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/stop-trump-from-destabilizing-markets")**

**If Donald Trump and Mike Pence follow through on their threat to further disrupt health care markets, premiums in Indiana would skyrocket. Thousands of Hoosier families ' coverage status would be uncertain.**

Our fight to protect Hoosiers' health care far from over. I'm asking you to continue to stand with me and my colleagues in the Senate in telling Trump to follow the first rule of health care: do no harm.

**[Add your name: Demand the Administration stop its attempts to sabotage our health insurance markets.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2735/7377f838/375a9ed1/51fbd9e8/3812481161/VEsF/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/stop-trump-from-destabilizing-markets")**

Thank you,

Joe

subject: The State Fair
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
|
---
**_The State Fair_**
As the former Chairman of the Indiana State Fair Commission, I can't help but point out that the Indiana State Fair kicked off today. But more important than that, the Indiana Republican Party's State Fair Booth is now open for business! We encourage any and all Indiana Republicans attending the fair to stop by to say hello anytime from 10am-10pm from today through August 20th. The Booth is located just north of the covered bridge across from the Free Stage. Our team will be there every day registering voters, sharing news and information about our candidates and elected officials and giving away some fun prizes. Don't forget, if you'd like to volunteer at the booth you can contact **James Cecil** at [jcecil@indiana.gop](mailto:jcecil@indiana.gop). There are plenty of volunteer shifts still available. It's a fun time and a great cause.

Also, mark your calendars for **Eric Holcomb** Day at the Booth on August 12th and Statewides Day with **Connie Lawson** , **Kelly Mitchell** and **Tera Klutz** on August 19th. More information to follow on both events.
\- Kyle

(Photo: Assistant to the Secretary **Danielle Zagone** and volunteer **Molly Miller** setting up the Indiana Republican Party Booth at the Indiana State Fair this morning.)
|
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
|
---
---
| "Thriving." That's the one word [_Governing Magazine_ used](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a19e5bf80a&e=1acd9bb3d6) to describe Governor Eric Holcomb's first six months in office. Here's part of their reasoning: "Observers credit him with bringing strong appointees into his administration and for taking an active yet unpretentious role in shaping legislation. He helped enact a 10-year road funding and maintenance plan, and he's prioritized some long-lagging concerns, such as information technology modernization." Obviously, the list of reasons is much, much longer than these two sentences suggest but we couldn't agree more with their one-word analysis. And when Governor Holcomb is thriving, Indiana is too!

As usual, Governor Holcomb found himself on the road a lot this week. He started it off by spending quality time with [bona fide American heroes](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9f8296abfc&e=1acd9bb3d6) at the 72nd reunion of USS Indianapolis survivors. He then made stops in Central Indiana [alongside EPA Administrator Scott Pruitt](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f07922e2c2&e=1acd9bb3d6) and in Howard County for their [annual Lincoln Day Dinner](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f26950c533&e=1acd9bb3d6). A big thanks goes to Chairman **Martin Stout** for the invitation and Commissioner **Paul Wyman** for moderating an entertaining and informative discussion with the Governor.
---
|
---
|
---
| _**Indiana GOP Seeking Fall Interns**_
We are currently accepting applications for one of the BEST political internship programs in the state! If you or someone you know is interested in learning about Communications, Grassroots and/or Fundraising from the perspective of a statewide political party, make sure they apply today. And if you need more convincing about our internship program, just take a look at what our current interns - [**David Boyer**](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=51a99379a0&e=1acd9bb3d6), [**James Cecil**](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fd46c3d710&e=1acd9bb3d6), [**Michael Jefferies**](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=563cba6d7f&e=1acd9bb3d6), [**Delaney Parker**](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4af5fc039a&e=1acd9bb3d6) and [**Luke Thomas**](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8dc77942e8&e=1acd9bb3d6) - have to say about their experience with us this summer. For more information, contact **Derek Hugo** at dhugo@indiana.gop or visit our internship page [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9ed9ccb731&e=1acd9bb3d6).
---
|
---
|
---
_**Indiana's Dr. Jerome Adams Confirmed as 20th U.S. Surgeon General**_
Dr. **Jerome Adams** , most recently Indiana's Health Commissioner, was confirmed on Thursday to serve as our nation's 20th Surgeon General. Vice President **Mike Pence** , Director of National Intelligence **Dan Coats** , CMS Administrator **Seema Verma** , soon-to-be Deputy Administrator of the Federal Highway Administration **Brandye Hendrickson** , soon-to-be Under Secretary of Trade and Foreign Agricultural Affairs **Ted McKinney** , and now Surgeon General Jerome Adams all have one thing in common...they are Hoosiers who made Indiana proud and are now making our entire nation proud!
|
---
| **News You Can Use**
**Governor Eric Holcomb**[gushes over his love for the Cone Palace](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b218c815cc&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**[discussed the five pillars at the Michigan City Rotary](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c02490afb0&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Jackie Walorski**[introduced a bill to improve access to air medical services](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=182f73d45f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Jim Banks**[held a town hall meeting in Auburn](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c2591eeb8a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Larry Bucshon**[statement on sanctions legislation](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d492e5640b&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**News** : [Allegiant plans aircraft base in Indiana, new jobs and future growth to follow](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=44f72415c5&e=1acd9bb3d6)

clean_body: | | | | | | The State Fair As the former Chairman of the Indiana State Fair Commission, I can't help but point out that the Indiana State Fair kicked off today. But more important than that, the Indiana Republican Party's State Fair Booth is now open for business! We encourage any and all Indiana Republicans attending the fair to stop by to say hello anytime from 10am-10pm from today through August 20th. The Booth is located just north of the covered bridge across from the Free Stage. Our team will be there every day registering voters, sharing news and information about our candidates and elected officials and giving away some fun prizes. Don't forget, if you'd like to volunteer at the booth you can contact James Cecil at mailto:jcecil@indiana.gop. There are plenty of volunteer shifts still available. It's a fun time and a great cause. Also, mark your calendars for Eric Holcomb Day at the Booth on August 12th and Statewides Day with Connie Lawson , Kelly Mitchell and Tera Klutz on August 19th. More information to follow on both events. \- Kyle (Photo: Assistant to the Secretary Danielle Zagone and volunteer Molly Miller setting up the Indiana Republican Party Booth at the Indiana State Fair this morning.) | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | | "Thriving." That's the one word to describe Governor Eric Holcomb's first six months in office. Here's part of their reasoning: "Observers credit him with bringing strong appointees into his administration and for taking an active yet unpretentious role in shaping legislation. He helped enact a 10-year road funding and maintenance plan, and he's prioritized some long-lagging concerns, such as information technology modernization." Obviously, the list of reasons is much, much longer than these two sentences suggest but we couldn't agree more with their one-word analysis. And when Governor Holcomb is thriving, Indiana is too! As usual, Governor Holcomb found himself on the road a lot this week. He started it off by spending quality time with at the 72nd reunion of USS Indianapolis survivors. He then made stops in Central Indiana and in Howard County for their . A big thanks goes to Chairman Martin Stout for the invitation and Commissioner Paul Wyman for moderating an entertaining and informative discussion with the Governor. | | | Indiana GOP Seeking Fall Interns We are currently accepting applications for one of the BEST political internship programs in the state! If you or someone you know is interested in learning about Communications, Grassroots and/or Fundraising from the perspective of a statewide political party, make sure they apply today. And if you need more convincing about our internship program, just take a look at what our current interns - , , , and - have to say about their experience with us this summer. For more information, contact Derek Hugo at dhugo@indiana.gop or visit our internship page . | | Indiana's Dr. Jerome Adams Confirmed as 20th U.S. Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams , most recently Indiana's Health Commissioner, was confirmed on Thursday to serve as our nation's 20th Surgeon General. Vice President Mike Pence , Director of National Intelligence Dan Coats , CMS Administrator Seema Verma , soon-to-be Deputy Administrator of the Federal Highway Administration Brandye Hendrickson , soon-to-be Under Secretary of Trade and Foreign Agricultural Affairs Ted McKinney , and now Surgeon General Jerome Adams all have one thing in common...they are Hoosiers who made Indiana proud and are now making our entire nation proud! | | News You Can Use Governor Eric Holcomb Lt. name: Republican Party of New Hampshire
subject: NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on Secretary Shulkin Visit
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: August 4, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310

Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Governor Sununu
and Secretary Shulkin Manchester VA visit

Concord, NH – NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the
following statement regarding Governor Sununu’s and VA Secretary
Shulkin's visit to the Manchester VA Medical Center:

“It is inspiring to see Governor Sununu and Veterans
Administration Secretary Shulkin take matters into their own
hands to ensure that our New Hampshire veterans receive the best
care possible. Governor Sununu is fighting to ensure that New
Hampshire veterans receive the care that they deserve—the best
care. We welcome Secretary Shulkin to New Hampshire and want him
to know that working with Governor Sununu will achieve real
reform our veterans deserve.”

###

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, August 4th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| **Dear Derek,**
**On Wednesday evening I attended a meeting of Republicans in Franklin working to establish a new committee at the grassroots level. It is exciting and encouraging to know that all around the state, Republicans are working tirelessly to grow our base.**
**We have great existing committees that are out there recruiting new members and spreading the word about Republican policies and beliefs through social media, letters, events and word of mouth. And, we need to do more of that!**
**For the first time in over a decade we control the corner office and Governor Chris Sununu is one of the most popular governors in the country. In this last session the legislature and the Governor working together had some amazing successes: a fiscally sound budget that sets important priorities, improving voter integrity, constitutional carry, and passing a fetal homicide law. As former Governor John H. Sununu says, we need to “talk, talk, talk” about our successes (because rest assured, the Democrats won’t!).**
**To that end, expanding our local Republican committees and establishing new ones will be critically important as we move towards 2018. We must grow our local presence to compete with the Democrats’ DC-money machine.**
**So folks, this is a call to action – won’t you help in your community? Reach out and invite some new people to your next committee meeting. If you don’t have a local Republican committee, call our office and we’ll help you get started. And make sure to mark you calendar for the first R.E.D. Summit, Saturday, September 30 th at St. Anselm College. We will be training folks to do just that – make a political difference in YOUR community!**
**Sincerely,**
**_Jeanie_**

clean_body: | | | NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, August 4th | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, On Wednesday evening I attended a meeting of Republicans in Franklin working to establish a new committee at the grassroots level. It is exciting and encouraging to know that all around the state, Republicans are working tirelessly to grow our base. We have great existing committees that are out there recruiting new members and spreading the word about Republican policies and beliefs through social media, letters, events and word of mouth. And, we need to do more of that! For the first time in over a decade we control the corner office and Governor Chris Sununu is one of the most popular governors in the country. In this last session the legislature and the Governor working together had some amazing successes: a fiscally sound budget that sets important priorities, improving voter integrity, constitutional carry, and passing a fetal homicide law. As former Governor John H. Sununu says, we need to “talk, talk, talk” about our successes (because rest assured, the Democrats won’t!). To that end, expanding our local Republican committees and establishing new ones will be critically important as we move towards 2018. We must grow our local presence to compete with the Democrats’ DC-money machine. So folks, this is a call to action – won’t you help in your community? Reach out and invite some new people to your next committee meeting. If you don’t have a local Republican committee, call our office and we’ll help you get started. And make sure to mark you calendar for the first R.E.D. Summit, Saturday, September 30 th at St. Anselm College. We will be training folks to do just that – make a political difference in YOUR community!
